[u] Straight from installation: [/u] is SH5 playable? Yes, it is to an extent. [u] Is it stable? [/u] Yes, it runs just fine on windows 10. [b] However [/b] many inconcistencies exist gameplaywise which may as well make this game the same as any other CTD riddled mess [i] (such as the ship recognition interface being written with completely incorrect dimensions regarding ship mast heights, draft depths, vessel lengths and beam (width)) [/i]. There exist certain coding issues [i] (while rare, a possibility for a bug making all torpedoes fired by the player treated as duds, regardless of the relevant gameplay option being enabled or disabled) [/i]. Another possibility exists that may bar the player from ever progressing beyond the starting campaign begining in 1939. Now, these are [u] only possibilities [/u] and [u] not always encountered, [/u] but these have been encountered often enough to come to the attention of many. There exist many mods that fix many of these issues and enhance gameplay. Hell, I couldnt stand this game until downloading and installing a megamod hosted by the subsim community known as The Wolves Of Steel. [u] So: do I reccommend this game right out of the box? [/u] [i] Absolutely not. [/i] After modding the inherrent bugs, glitches, and recursive coding issues out of existence? Shit yeah! This becomes an excellent Uboot simulator after fixing it up, but patience is a definite must played vanilla or modded

I really wanted to like this game, but I just couldn't for two major reasons:
1. After playing the campaign for about 12 hours, I got to a spot where whatever I do (besides loading an older save) crashes the game. So, lots of my campaign progress has been lost.
2. There are tons of bugs in the game, some minor, some not so minor. For example, sometimes sounds just stop working for no good reason, sometimes functionality just stops working (e.g., depth under keel). And, oh... the periscope view using the default periscope depth is next to useless as most times the periscope is under water... ugh.

Add to all that the just completely useless and stupid Ubisoft launcher (why can't I just lanuch from Steam! in 2018?) and I have given up on this game. I really enjoyed the idea and some of the gameplay, but the game is just a mess.

OK, so maybe I should have gotten the Wolves of Steel mega-mod (which requies an account on subsim), but really I need to mod the heck out of the game to make it functional?

Ok. The ghame is old, but still ...

Let's start with the annoying stuff.
The game doesn't save your settings, like the size/poision of the minimap or the tutorial tips for some controls will pop up EVERY single time you start/load a game or even when entering/leaving the port.
Even though the game only has like a dozen hotkeys assigned, those are spread all over the keyboard and no ... you cannot change them. There is only a "help" in game showing a US layout keyboard with where the hotkeys are ... good luck if you don't have the US layout or no keypad...
You have to travel thousands of miles, for that you have time compression. But you have to hit the time compression button a dozen times to have any useful value and any spotted ship/plane/shallow waters/ a seagull shitting on your conning tower will completely reset it ... nothing you can do about it. Even worse you set waypoints ... then die because your boat beached on a 1m² island because the nonexistent AI doesn't warn nor bother evading. Naturally that "island" can only be seen if you completely zoom in to the map, so everytime you set a 20000 miles course you have to waste 30minutes checking every inch of the course with max zoom to make sure there isn't a pile of dirt on the course.
Talking about AI ... i have yet to see hostile planes do anything and i have yet to see any hostile ship cause any damage except for firing his main guns while i'm surfaced...
As you play your Crew members can unlock abilities, including active ones. But in order to activate the ability you actually have to walk to the officer and talk to him...
You can manually fire the deck gun and AA. Manning the AA is easy, manning the deck gun not. You cannot leave the conning tower and the tower blocks your line of sight to the gun. So you have to maniacally move your mouse around praying for the 2 visible pixel of the barrel to be highlighted indicating that you can man the gun with leftclick...

Now for the for gamebreaking stuff.
When i started the game it automatically closed without any notice. Ok, I'm using Win10 64Bit and the game is old ... some searching the web i found out i have to manually edit some settings ... too bad the posts describing that are basically as old as the game itself.
Even though the game now runs, it still occasionally closes without any warning or notice.
Hotkeys, especially the ones for manning the deck gun / AA tend to not work at all. You actually have to walk to the conning tower and talk to the officer to get the guns mounted, unmounting the guns via hotkey works though...
Even though the savegame directly lists special ships and their status (active/sunk) it doesn't work, by now i have sunk the HMS Rodney, HMS Queen Elizabeth and HMS Hood at least 4 times in a single mission...
On my first trip to the port of Dover there was some dozen ship wrecks happily skipping on the water surface ...
Every one in a while when you surface your deck officer (and the spotters) refuse to man the conning tower ... which means you can't talk to him to man the guns ... only way to fix it is by restarting the game.
Winning missions is nearly impossible, for qhatever stupid reason kills are not contributed to mission objectives. Only way to "fix" it is by manually editing your save file.


Various posts and threads confirm that those problems and bugs have been around since release. So it heavily looks like Ubisoft just sold a beta and abandoned it right away.
On a sidenote there are mods, supposedly fixing a good amount of things. I didn't try them out. For me a game that requires some dozen mods to be playable,let alone enjoyable, is just crap ... especially if the game doesn't even directly support modding.

If you want a sub game, just play it's predecessors ... or different games. Silent Hunter 5 is garbage ... at best

EDITED REVIEW


EDIT:  I don't know what part of me said to give it a shot, but I shouldn't have listened to it.  The game simply is too buggy to enjoy.  Case in point:  One of the missions starting out is to find an aircraft carrier patroling the atlantic and sink it.  No big deal right?  So I go out on search for this thing, two in game months of false contacts and enemy patrols that don't consist of this thing go by and nothing.  Finally I see it, crusing away at twice the speed my sub can move off into the distance.  A while later I see it again, this time closing on me.  I launch every torpedo I have at this thing and get 2 hits, the health bar is low and the flooding bar is filling up, I've done it.  Then the flooding just stops.  I wait out the destroyers and surface.  It's listing and has water over the flight deck, but not sinking.  I fire every one of my 88 shells at it and not a scratch, there it floats.  I make a note of where it is and go sail to the closest resupply spot and go back to finish it once and for all.  It's gone.





The controls are completely different from pervious games and you have to pretty much fumble around yourself to learn them.  I can't even get past the first mission in which you have to sink an aircraft carrier, I saw it once and couldn't catch up to it.  I sailed around for a month looking for it but it was never seen again.  Oh, and multiplayer is no longer available.  

All that being said give it a shot, I gave it one last chance after reviewing, and it does a lot of stuff right.  You can walk around your sub from front to back, customize your sub in more ways than before.  And the graphics are acceptable on even the most basic level.  As for the controls yes they're weird at first, but give it a half hour and you'll get the hang of it.  I think my first review was too harsh. i give it 7.9/10

Undoubtedly the worst in program writing. Expect major conflicts, and numerous program failures. They could not even get the little things right. Example: you sink ships in an area clearly marked yet the program fails to record it towards the mission. When you finally succeed in destroying enough shipping to have accomplished the mission, and the program says you have, it still tells you the mission is incomplete. Every time I click on the XO at a specific spot in front of the map table, the program exits and fails to save , so when you reboot everything is lost. What a POS. Isn't worth 2 cents, let alone $10. Don't waste your money.

Another "game" sh@ out by Ubisoft. IDK how products like these still manage to be on Steam.

First of all, the game doesn't even work for some people (especially those with Windows 10). I was lucky enough to have it working on my pc but damn, it's still terrible. The settings are pretty much useless because once you launch the campaign, it hovers at around 8-12fps. For reference, my graphics card is a GTX1070 paired with an i7-7700hq so I was expecting a game from 2010 to run without problems.
I even tried to lower the settings and all it did was make the game look worse and gave me 1 extra fps. Definitely not recommended to buy this floating turd of a game because to even play the game, you need to be a computer science major while also learning how to tinker with the game's files which shouldn't even be happening since this is a paid game.

If you are looking at this review right now and wondering if you should pay for this game, the answer is a big fat NO. Games like these don't deserve money and instead deserve to be pirated because there is literally no support from the devs and multiplayer is almost dead anyway.

I like to think im a frugle spender. The first silent hunter game I bought was 'Silent Hunter 3' for around £3-£4. I didnt expect much from an old, cheap game but, My God, was I impressed! for a game that came out when I was 7 its beautiful. I then bought the package for around £25. I imediatly installed 'Silent Hunter 4' and was again blown away!

 Then came 'Silent Hunter 5'. Its not just a game or a Simulator. Its a window into the lives of the crews of the Submarines of World War 2! 
Each of the main crew members has a back story. They all have their own reasons for joining the Kreigsmarine. They are'nt all raving, racist Nazis (bar one). They're all human, and you can really identify with many of them. 

The Graphics are excellent and the gameplay is so realistic it blows my mind. I've had to actually use the maths I spent most of my childhood learning at school to play this game. And that was only optional! 

If you're a history nerd like me and are interested in the human side of WW2 as well as being a Submarine lover then buy this game!

Ok, there are a lot of bad reviews and there can be difficulties getting this game to run, that's true. But if one follows the advice given on subsim.com and installs the Wolves of Steel MegaMod, this game becomes a gem and feels a lot like SH3 with GWX.

I haven't played it much yet though, I first had to get it to run - one problem was for example, that it didn't start at all at first and Uplay crashed - reinstalling Uplay solved this. I had to make a few changes to the compatibility tab of the game executables (related to DPI, run as admin and windows 7 compatibility mode), but then it worked.

I can recommend it to those familiar with SH3 with GWX (as SH5 with Wolves of Steel installed), but not to those, who are not familiar with playing Silent Hunter,, because the manual of SH5 is ridiculous and Wolves of Steel does as well not have a manual (in contrast to GWX mod with SH3, which has a very extensive manual).

The Wolves of Steel mod is very good though and has lots of options - you can even use an Enigma machine to decode radio transmissions from BdU if you want to. The progression system is changed in pleasant ways, overall I know already that I will spend hundreds of hours with this game - I love SH3 with GWX, but SH5 with Wolves of Steel has advanced features, which might even be better in certain play styles - but it takes some effort to get into it.

This is actually what I did and why my play time is nearly non-existant for now - I watched a couple of videos on Youtube to decide, how much of realism i want. Wolves of Steel has even realistic navigation, where your boat and heading is not on the map at all, you have to figure that out yourself where you are and where you're heading - I like that a lot.

So, yes, can be recommended, but not for those, who do not want to put in the effort to get it to run - it might take some effort to figure that out. And I highly recommend to use Wolves of Steel, otherwise the game play is odd.

Das Boot: The Game.

Some old fans of the series would recommend SH3 as the best of the series, but I think that's just nostalgia talking. This is the best u-boat simulator out there in my opinion. It's very atmospheric and intense, unlike a lot of cold and sterile sims. The game strikes a good balance between realism and playability, and in-game settings and mods can be used to make the game more/less realistic to suit.

Highly recommended to anyone with even a remote interest in submarine warfare.

The Best submarine game ever

like all silent hunter games.. this is HARD... it is brutal... it is realistic... you will die OFTEN... most submarine crews DIDNT make it. its why they had the respect of both sides.. even after the war, americans still respected and feared german submarine crews. brave and deadly.. and near suicidal insanity. torpedos are NOT the main weapon. this isnt some movie. torps stop a target or slow them enough for you to get away or finish the job with the deck gun. and german gun crews were some of the best in the world. use your wits, your skills, your luckiest of trinkets, and a few well placed prayers and you may live long enough to be one of the best. if luck, fate, the gods, or training are against you, then you will join the merchant ships you sunk in Davy Jones' locker. run silent, run deep, and may the sea have mercy on you.

I have played other silent hunter games. this one basicly includes the features we modded into the earlier ones. great action, dificult foes, and even some history. for a fun game, turn it all the way to easy and enjoy the frag fest.. for the ultimate challenge, turn it to full realistic and see why even some of the best commanders ended their carreers on the bottom in crushed and blown out hulls.

After taking a few years break and revisiting, it's still the worst game ever. Poorly defined mission success criteria, buggy things like CO2 levels not resetting when surfaced, marks on the map disappearing after reloading a saved game.  Seemingly the only way to actually play the game is to spend buttloads of time researching what community mods might possibly help (but it's not guaranteed), and then experimenting.  A waste of money, avoid!

I know, I know ...

The game is busted.  It doesn't work.  Bugs all over the place.  Don't buy it ...

... and so on, and so forth.

However, in years past, I've played this game for hours on end, without any bugs, no CTDs, nothing.  So, how did I do it?

Mods.  Mods are the key to just about every game from Ubisoft (which is not one of my favorite game publishers, but they do have some great ideas).  Specifically, the Wolves of Steel (TWoS), which can be found at SubSim.com.  How good is it?  At times, you would swear that you are the Kapitanleutnant in the movie "Das Boot".  I've devoured that movie (including the 5-hour version of it), and I love TWoS when connected with Silent Hunter 5.

You start the campaign game in the Eastern Baltic, at a port which was Hitler's last bloodless conquest.  War has just broken out against Poland.  At least, it is just about to break out:  the date is August 31, 1939, and there is the Polish Navy to deal with.

After you clear the port, you go on an inspection tour of your "Boot" - short for "Unterseeboot".  You climb down the ladder into the conning tower, and then into the control (while your crew stands watch on the bridge).  You speak to the navigator, the Chief, and go forward through the hatch and speak to your radio operator and sound man.  If you turn around, you'll see your own cabin (a tiny little place with a curtain, your "desk", and your bunk).  Just forward of that location in the ward room, some crew quarters, and the torpedo room.  Going aft, you find some more crew quarters, the tiny galley (which was supposed to prepare food for 53 crewmen), the engine room, and the electric motor room.

If you've ever watched "Das Boot", all of these locations look eerily familiar (and by the way, I've toured the U-555 which is currently on display at the Museum of Science and Industry, in Chicago.

As you continue your operations against the Polish, the British, and all of Germany's enemies (later to include the United States), and you are pursued by aircraft and destroyers, you may feel a surge of adrenaline, increased heart rate ... you feel like you are there.  The game gets harder, as the years pass, and if you survive.

The graphics are beautiful, the gameplay is seamless, and it functions like Ubisoft SHOULD HAVE made it function.

To make it work right, you simply have to get the Wolves of Steel mod.  It's free.  And the basic game costs only $10, so what have you got to lose?

If you have any interest at all, in WW2 naval warfare, and specifically in the U-boat campaign in the Atlantic, you simply have to buy this game.

Strongly recommended ....

After-Action report:
Just two nights ago, I guided my Type VIIb boat into the Gulf of Danzig (using The Wolves of Steel megamod), and found a Polish taskforce consisting of two destroyers.  Although I approached them with decks awash (to lower my profile on the horizon) a lookout must have seen us.  The bottom was 67 meters, so I went to 50 meters depth and went silent.

Depth charges were all over the place, and we got shook up pretty badly, but took no serious damage.  Suddenly, the depth charge attacks ended.  I judged that the destroyers had run out them, and took my boat up to periscope depth.  Carefully edging the attack scope up, till it just broke the surface.  I saw one of the destroyers about 500 meters off.  I made a quick resolution of the attack and sent two torpedoes its way, aiming just below the bridge.  Both hit, and the destroyer went down quickly.

Meanwhile, the other destroyer started to run away, but he was very close, also.  I made a snapshot with tube 3 & 4, and one of them hit.  When tube one was loaded, I hit him, again.  He went down very fast. 

I'm now on my way to my new base, at Kiel.  In the interim, I sank two Polish merchant vessels, one with a torpedo, and one with gunfire.

Steam should be ashamed for selling a known defective product.

After replaying the first section of the campaign 3 times due to a "glitch" which prevents progression, some research brought me to several forums which all indicated that the Steam version of the game is inexorably broken and the DRM on the steam version of the .exe prevents it from being patched by the official ubisoft release.

Of course Steam won't refund you after you've played the game for x number of hours even if it takes that long to realize the game is broken. I'll remember to buy my games from GOG going forward.

It isn't everything it's hyped up to be. If you are an impatient person, you don't wanna play this game. However, if you are a strategical lover of realistic World War II games then this is for you. Personally I am one of those people. Please note, this game may be decieving. It may only be 3.8GB but trust me... you'll want to blow your brains out of boredom while traveling halfway across the globe to your next mission. HAVE FUN!!!

The game is broken, your boat sinks out of nothing even on easy mode. And singleplayer is impossible to complete because the game does not register the correct amount of tonnage you sunk. Its ver buggy.

Really don't understand the crying of some people here. I never encountered a bug when I've played the game.

An absolute pain to play using an AZERTY keyboard as all the keybindings are for QWERTY, no way to change the keybindings either unless you want to download more mods. The only way I could get the game to use a AZERTY keyboard is when I changed the language to German or French, which I don't speak. This one small thing ruined this game for me.

Ok, So what is Silent Hunter 5 Battle of the Atlantic, Well it's game based upon the adventures of Captain German McUboot and his crew of merry men as they go fight the evil British whle also making Bratwurst and Schnitzel... Or atleast that's what I got from playing the game for about 4 hours. Yeah 4 hours I spent 2 of em trying to get the damn game running.

This game is pretty much deader then a T2 Tanker being hit by 4 T-3 Torpedoes while it is fully loaded with Kerostene, Support is dead, Modding community is on a DNR by the community and that's why this game is in a bundle with in my opinion 2 much better games (Note: I have 39h on SH3 not including modded game playtime and 100 hours on SH4) now since Ubisoft know people won't buy this game.

Would I recommend picking the bundle for Silent Hunter 5, That's a big fat NO.

Would I recommend picking the bundle for Silent Hunter 3 and 4 (Wolves of the Pacific): No.

Would I recommend picking up Silent Hunter 3 and 4 on their own: Yes. (Trust me I'm saving you money here by buying the games on their own)

Now, seriously, this game is fucking buggy. The gameplay and graphics are very nice, but this damn mission is on the southwestern shore of England. unless you are prepared to do a 12 hours long session going back and forth through the the damn Norwegian Sea ([i]believe me, it sucks, it takes time and it's boring[/i]), you won't get past the first mission. Also, don't bother trying to leave the game open just so you don't have to load and get bugged, as your game will crash after some time. Also, good luck not having your crew dying of CO2 intoxication just because the game bugged and it thinks you are submerged when you are in fact breathing fresh morning air on the French coast.

Ubisoft don't give a shit about you.

They abandoned the game and will never return. Also, if you want MODS that might fix the problem, don't buy the Steam version.

Move along, this is not the U-Boat Sim you are looking for...
